threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it a 'tube' instead of the 'Overground'—big giveaway you're not yet part of the Dalston vibe.
Caught a bloke singing in French on the 333 last night, totally in his own world. Brixton's night bus vibe is like nowhere else.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a pop-up flea market on Sonnenallee, with everything from second-hand records to homemade hummus. That’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you order a currywurst at 3 AM from a street vendor while listening to a spontaneous live DJ set. It's a vibe like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they don’t even blink at a BART evacuation, just keep scrolling on their phone like it’s a Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: The other day, a dog just chilled on the tracks at Civic Center. Muni was stuck for ages while everyone snapped pics. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just grab a pa amb tomoquet anywhere, but the real gem is from that little bar on Carrer de Verdi, made by the old guy with the mustache.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll catch them checking the T10 ticket like it's some kind of mystical artifact, still unsure if they've punched it correctly before getting on the metro.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused by the 149 bus route - you stop too long at stops, waiting for the next one while locals hop on and off without glancing.
Caught a late 59 last night, and a guy in a bright pink onesie was singing old school grime. The whole bus was in stitches.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I lived here was when I saw an old man playing an accordion next to a street vendor selling vegan kebabs at Rathaus Neukölln. That mix says it all.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find currywurst stands playing techno at 3 AM, where lines of locals and tourists seamlessly blend under flickering neon.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they know the perfect dodgy taco truck parked at 18th & Mission, but avoid it after dark.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus stop with a ukulele while waiting for the 19. Only in San Francisco do your delays come with a soundtrack.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they know the best spots, but the best vermut isn’t in a fancy bar; it’s at that little bodega on Carrer de les Progrés, always buzzing with locals.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still stop at crosswalks, waiting for the elusive pedestrian right of way. Locals just stroll on through.
